title: [LLHD] HoistSignals crash on multiple drives with different delays
body:
The following snippet crashes `circt-opt --llhd-hoist-signals`:
```mlir
hw.module private @foo(in %b : i42) {
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <1ns, 0d, 0e>
  %1 = llhd.constant_time <2ns, 0d, 0e>
  %a = llhd.sig %b : i42
  llhd.process {
    llhd.drv %a, %b after %0 : i42
    llhd.wait delay %0, ^bb1
  ^bb1:
    llhd.drv %a, %b after %1 : i42
    llhd.wait delay %0, ^bb2
  ^bb2:
    llhd.halt
  }
  hw.output
}
```
This causes a call to `hw::getBitWidth` to return a value below 0:
```
circt-opt: lib/Dialect/LLHD/Transforms/HoistSignals.cpp:504: 
  auto (anonymous namespace)::DriveHoister::hoistDrives()
    ::(anonymous class)::operator()(DriveValue)
    ::(anonymous class)::operator()(auto) const [type:auto = mlir::Type]:
  Assertion `numBits >= 0' failed.
```
Relevant lines: https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/main/lib/Dialect/LLHD/Transforms/HoistSignals.cpp#L502-L504. The problem goes away if the `llhd.drv` ops all have the same delay.

Some of the [segfaults in sv-tests](https://github.com/circt/circt-tests/blob/results/2025/2025-12-15-131140-main-82227de/sv-tests/segfaults.txt) are related to this.
code_blocks:
hw.module private @foo(in %b : i42) {
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <1ns, 0d, 0e>
  %1 = llhd.constant_time <2ns, 0d, 0e>
  %a = llhd.sig %b : i42
  llhd.process {
    llhd.drv %a, %b after %0 : i42
    llhd.wait delay %0, ^bb1
  ^bb1:
    llhd.drv %a, %b after %1 : i42
    llhd.wait delay %0, ^bb2
  ^bb2:
    llhd.halt
  }
  hw.output
}
circt-opt: lib/Dialect/LLHD/Transforms/HoistSignals.cpp:504: 
  auto (anonymous namespace)::DriveHoister::hoistDrives()
    ::(anonymous class)::operator()(DriveValue)
    ::(anonymous class)::operator()(auto) const [type:auto = mlir::Type]:
  Assertion `numBits >= 0' failed.
